response.justification: While the candidate brings breadth across many domains and has genuine geospatial/GIS exposure through GISCollective and Green Map projects, the core technical requirements for this role are largely unmet. The position demands strong modern C++ or Rust expertise for a performance-critical routing data pipeline; the candidate's primary stack is TypeScript/Node.js/DLang, with C++ usage limited to a brief stint at National Instruments in 2014–2015 and a bachelor thesis project. There is no evidence of routing tile generation, navigation guidance systems, or pipeline latency optimization at the systems level this role requires.
strengths: Genuine geospatial/GIS experience through the GISCollective open-source platform and Green Map web GIS application
Demonstrated low-latency, high-throughput systems work at Dunnhumby handling millions of HTTP requests per second using async I/O and high-performance databases
Broad distributed systems exposure and Linux proficiency across multiple commercial roles
concerns: No modern C++ or Rust experience; C++ use is dated (2014–2015) and limited in scope, with no indication of performance-critical C++ at scale
No experience with routing pipelines, tile generation, navigation guidance, or map data transformation — the core domain of this role
Career trajectory points firmly toward web/TypeScript full-stack work, diverging further from the systems programming this position requires
